name: Customer Support
tagline: Draft accurate replies, triage tickets, and pull answers from your own docs without exposing customer data.
heroTitle: Faster replies, grounded in your real answers.
heroSubtitle: Agents read a ticket, find the right answer in your help center and past resolutions, and draft a reply your team approves. They connect to your help desk to tag, route, and update, and the Gateway redacts customer PII before anything leaves your machine.
intro: Support is a volume game where accuracy and trust both matter. A generic chatbot invents answers and leaks customer data; your team needs replies grounded in your real knowledge base and a clear record of what was sent. Ryu grounds agents on your docs, connects them to your help desk, and keeps PII redacted on every model call.

highlights.title: Answers from your docs
highlights.description: Agents pull from your help center, macros, and past tickets with Spaces and RAG, so replies cite a real source.

highlights.title: Customer data stays safe
highlights.description: The Gateway redacts names, emails, and account details on egress, and can run fully local for sensitive queues.

highlights.title: Wired into your help desk
highlights.description: Connect to Zendesk, Intercom, or your stack to tag, route, and update tickets, not just suggest text.

highlights.title: Human stays in the loop
highlights.description: Every draft is a suggestion your agent reviews and sends, with the reasoning and sources visible.

useCases.title: Reply drafting
useCases.description: Read the ticket, find the matching article or past resolution, and draft a reply in your support voice for review.

useCases.title: Ticket triage and routing
useCases.description: Classify incoming tickets by topic, urgency, and sentiment, then tag and route them to the right queue or person.

useCases.title: Knowledge base search
useCases.description: Ask plain questions across your docs and macros; get the answer plus a link to the exact article it came from.

useCases.title: Escalation summaries
useCases.description: Bundle a messy thread into a clean summary with the issue, steps tried, and account context for the next tier.

useCases.title: Macro and article drafting
useCases.description: Spot repeat questions in recent tickets and draft new help articles or canned responses to cover them.

useCases.title: Follow-up and CSAT chasing
useCases.description: Draft polite follow-ups on stalled tickets and post-resolution check-ins, queued for an agent to send.

examplePrompts: Read this ticket and draft a reply, citing the help article it's based on.
Triage the last 50 open tickets by topic and urgency, and tell me which need a human now.
Summarize this 12-message thread into an escalation note with the issue, steps tried, and account details.
We keep getting asked about refunds, draft a help-center article and a canned macro for it.
Find every ticket from this week mentioning the export bug and group them for the engineering handoff.

faq.q: Where do the answers come from?
faq.a: From your own knowledge base, macros, and past resolutions, grounded with Spaces and RAG. Replies cite the article they came from so your agent can verify before sending, instead of trusting a guess.
Yes. The Gateway redacts PII like names, emails, and account numbers on egress, logs every call, and can route sensitive queues to a fully local model so nothing leaves your device.

faq.q: Will it auto-send replies to customers?
faq.a: Only if you choose to. By default agents draft and your team reviews and sends. You can keep a human in the loop for every reply or automate low-risk ones once you trust the workflow.

ctaTitle: Clear the queue without cutting corners.
ctaSubtitle: Grounded drafts, clean triage, and protected customer data, all reviewed by your team.
ctaNote: Grounded answers · PII redaction · Help-desk connected
